summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the TLS certificate verification bypass in dcap-qvl's collateral fetching allows connections to HTTPS servers presenting self-signed certificates. The reproduction starts a mock PCCS server with a self-signed certificate and demonstrates that a client configured with danger_accept_invalid_certs(true) (the same setting used by the library) successfully connects, while a secure client with default TLS verification rejects the same certificate.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should prove that (1) a client using the same reqwest configuration as dcap-qvl (danger_accept_invalid_certs(true)) can connect to an HTTPS server with a self-signed certificate, and (2) a secure client with default TLS verification rejects the same self-signed certificate. This demonstrates that an attacker performing MITM can intercept collateral requests without detection.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, started a mock HTTPS server with a self-signed certificate, and confirmed that the insecure client (matching dcap-qvl's configuration) successfully connected and received data from the server. The secure client correctly rejected the self-signed certificate with a certificate verification error. The source code at /target-repo/src/collateral.rs was verified to contain the danger_accept_invalid_certs(true) call.
verification_method: The verify.sh script performs three checks: (1) it greps the mounted source code at /target-repo/src/collateral.rs for the exact vulnerability marker 'danger_accept_invalid_certs(true)', (2) it starts a Python HTTPS server with a self-signed certificate and confirms the insecure client can connect (HTTP 200), and (3) it confirms the secure client rejects the same server with a certificate verification error. The exploit succeeds only when the insecure connection succeeds AND the secure connection fails, proving the TLS bypass exists.
evidence_produced_by_run: grep of /target-repo/src/collateral.rs confirms danger_accept_invalid_certs(true) is present at the vulnerable code location.
The insecure SSL client (verify_mode=CERT_NONE, mimicking the library's configuration) successfully connected to the mock HTTPS server and received a 200 OK response with mock collateral data.
The secure SSL client (default verify_mode=CERT_REQUIRED) rejected the same server with an SSLError/CertificateError, confirming that proper TLS verification would catch this MITM.
The contrast between the two results proves that dcap-qvl's disabled TLS verification allows connections that should be rejected.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
